title: Tangy Fluffy Turkish Shrimp Pasta
description: A bright, saffron‑hued tagliatelle tossed with succulent shrimp, tangy yogurt, and aromatic Turkish spices for a crowd‑pleasing, fluffy pasta.
instructions: In a large pot, bring salted water to a boil and cook tagliatelle until al dente, 8‑10 minutes; drain and set aside.
Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add minced garlic and chopped onion, sauté until softened, about 3 minutes.
Pour in tomato paste, stirring to coat the aromatics; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the shrimp to the skillet, seasoning with salt, pepper, paprika, and sumac. Cook, turning occasionally, until the shrimp turn pink, about 4 minutes.
Reduce heat to low, stir in yogurt and lemon juice, tossing to create a silky sauce that coats the shrimp. Let the sauce simmer for 2 minutes, allowing flavors to meld.
Gently fold the cooked tagliatelle into the skillet, ensuring each strand is coated with the creamy, tangy sauce.
Sprinkle freshly chopped dill over the pasta, stirring to distribute the herb aroma. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve immediately, optionally garnishing with additional dill or a drizzle of olive oil.
Enjoy a fluffy, tangy Turkish shrimp pasta that pairs perfectly with a crisp side salad or crusty bread.
